shell.fullscreenMobile: Full screen · more space (Esc to exit)
shell.hideMenuPanel: Hide menu panel · more space for content
shell.fullscreenDesktop: Full screen workspace · hides side menu for focus (Esc to exit)
shell.localeHint: Navigation and chrome only. Your courses and uploads stay as written.
dashboard.cmsWelcomeDefault: Welcome back — keep up the great work.
dashboard.cmsSubtitleDefault: Headlines here are editable by admins under CMS — no redeploy required.
dashboard.publicFamilyBody: Open the one-page site with hero, admissions, about us (and optional video), gallery, and contacts. School admins manage copy and hero/video in Admin → CMS and the default hero image in School settings.
dashboard.assessmentsCard.student: Published tests you can take in your enrolled courses (respects class or department targeting).
dashboard.assessmentsCard.parent: Unique published tests your linked children can access (combined across siblings).
dashboard.quickTipCmdK: Press ⌘K to search and jump anywhere.
dashboard.schoolWallEmpty: Nothing posted yet. Say hello to everyone in your school — students, teachers, and admins share this thread.
dashboard.discussionsEmptyStaff: No discussion posts yet. Chat appears on each course page.
dashboard.discussionsEmptyStudent: Enroll in a course to see and join discussions.
dashboard.reportCardEmpty: No graded submissions yet. Complete assessments from your courses to build your report card.
dashboard.certificatesStaff: Completion certificates are for students who finish all lessons in a course. See the Certificates page for details.
dashboard.certificatesEmpty: Complete every lesson in an enrolled course to unlock a certificate of completion.
dashboard.calendar.title: School calendar
dashboard.calendar.lead: Official dates, events, and dated quizzes & exams (when staff set open/due times).
dashboard.calendar.footerHint: Change month to load a wider date window. Linked quizzes and exams respect class or department targeting.
orgPages.messages.body: follow role rules: students with classmates, their course teachers, and org admins; teachers with other teachers, admins, and students in their courses; admins with all teachers and students.
orgPages.messages.wallTail: is still available for organization-wide announcements.
orgPages.library.title: Learning resource library
orgPages.library.lead: Central PDFs, videos, and links for your organization.
orgPages.library.adminNote: Admins see all resources, including unpublished.
orgPages.library.teacherNote: Teachers can add items and edit or remove only what they uploaded; published items are visible to everyone.
orgPages.reportCard.parentNoStudent: No student is linked to your parent account yet.
orgPages.reportCard.notPublishedBlurb: Report cards are not published right now. Your school administrator can turn them on under
orgPages.reportCard.subtitleParent: Submitted assessments and scores for your linked student.
orgPages.reportCard.subtitleStudent: Submitted assessments and scores across your courses.
orgPages.reportCard.emptySubtitle: Submitted assessments and scores across your courses in this organization.
orgPages.reportCard.fromEnrolledCourse: from a course you are enrolled in.
orgPages.cert.parentNoStudent: No student is linked to your parent account yet. Ask a school administrator to connect your account to your child's student profile.
orgPages.cert.introStaff: Certificates are issued to students who complete every lesson in an enrolled course.
orgPages.cert.introParent: Courses where your linked student has completed all lessons. Open a certificate to print or save as PDF.
orgPages.cert.introStudent: Courses where you have completed all lessons. Open a certificate to print or save as PDF.
orgPages.cert.verifyLead: Third parties can confirm a credential on the
orgPages.cert.staffHint: Switch to a student account or enroll as a student to earn completion certificates.
orgPages.cert.notPublished: Certificates are not published for students right now. Your school administrator can enable them under
orgPages.cert.emptyParent: No certificates yet for this student. When they finish every lesson in an enrolled course, it will appear here.
orgPages.cert.emptyStudent: No certificates yet. Finish every lesson in a course you are enrolled in, then return here.
orgPages.adminCalendar.lead: Resumption, closures, holidays, and school events appear on every member's dashboard. Quizzes and exams show there too when course staff add an
orgPages.adminCalendar.leadTail: on each assessment (continuous-assessment opens/due dates and exam windows).
orgPages.adminUsers.title: Users & invites
orgPages.adminUsers.lead: Invite teachers and students with a link. Suspend, edit roles, or remove members (teachers and students only). Other school admins are managed from the platform console.
orgPages.adminDepartments.title: Departments & faculty
orgPages.adminDepartments.lead: Academic departments, chairs, instructors, and student affiliations for higher-education organizations.
orgPages.adminDepartments.k12Only: Department management is intended for organizations set to
orgPages.adminDepartments.k12Tail: in School settings. K–12 schools use
orgPages.adminClasses.leadSecondary: Secondary form groups, optional track or pathway labels, and student rosters. Use each student's school email when adding.
orgPages.adminClasses.leadK12: Assign primary students to a class roster. Use the student's school email when adding.
orgPages.settings.noCurrentTerm: No current %s is set yet. Your administrator can add one under Admin → Academic terms.
orgPages.settings.notInClass: You are not assigned to a %s yet. Your administrator can add you under Admin → Classes & homerooms.
platform.subtitle: Cross-tenant console
auth.signInDescription: Use your school URL, email, and password.
auth.slugBeforeGoogle: Enter a valid school URL slug (e.g. demo-school) before using Google.
auth.slugMustMatch: Your school slug must match the organization you belong to.
auth.schoolSlugHelp: The path you use after /o/ — same as when you registered.
landing.carouselSubtitle: Swipe or use arrows — each card opens that school's public page.
landing.schoolCardTeaser: Admissions, about us, gallery & contacts — tap to open.
landing.installWebAppLead: Install this site as an app — full screen from your home screen, no store required.
landing.installWebAppIos: On iPhone or iPad: tap Share, then Add to Home Screen, then Add.
landing.installWebAppFallback: On Android, use Chrome and look for Install app in the menu. You can also open the menu (⋮) → Add to Home Screen.
landing.runningAsInstalledApp: You’re using the installed web app.
landing.defaultHeadline: Learning that feels as good as it runs
landing.defaultSubheadline: JWT auth, org URLs, assessments, resource library, and a polished dashboard — seed the demo and explore.
landing.feat1.title: Multi-tenant
landing.feat1.body: Each school at /o/your-slug with isolated data.
landing.feat2.title: Roles
landing.feat2.body: Admin, teacher, and student flows with JWT sessions.
landing.feat3.title: Assessments
landing.feat4.title: Library & blog
landing.feat4.body: Resources, headless CMS copy, and course chat.
auth.error.genericSignIn: Could not sign in. Check organization URL, email, and password.
auth.error.nonJsonResponse: The server did not return JSON (status %s). Often this means the API crashed — check the terminal (e.g. database down or JWT_SECRET too short).
auth.error.fetchNetwork: Could not reach this app's server. Confirm `npm run dev` is running and you're on the same URL (e.g. http://localhost:3000), not a different port or device name.
auth.error.genericCatch: Something went wrong signing in. Try again, or check the browser console and dev server logs.
auth.demoHintBody: Postgres must be running and `DATABASE_URL` must match it. Apply migrations, then seed: `npm run db:deploy` then `npm run db:seed`, or `npm run db:bootstrap` (Docker Postgres + deploy + seed). If you used `db:push` before or deploy errors on types already existing, run `npm run db:reset` (wipes local DB). Demo: slug `demo-school`, password `password123` — try `admin@test.com`, `teacher@test.com`, `student@test.com`, or `parent@test.com` (parent is linked to the demo student).
auth.oauth.unknown: Sign-in failed. Try again or contact support.
auth.oauth.google_no_account: No account in that school for this Google user. Accept an invite with the same email, then try Google again.
auth.oauth.google_not_configured: Google sign-in is not enabled on this server.
auth.oauth.google_invalid_state: Sign-in expired. Try Google sign-in again.
auth.oauth.google_invalid_org: Enter a valid school slug, then use Google sign-in.
auth.oauth.google_email_unverified: Verify your email in Google, then try again.
auth.oauth.google_link_conflict: This Google account is linked to a different user in that school.
auth.oauth.google_token_failed: Could not complete Google sign-in. Try again.
auth.oauth.google_profile_failed: Could not load your Google profile. Try again.
auth.oauth.suspended: This account has been suspended. Contact your school administrator.
auth.oauth.google_org_pending: This school is still waiting for platform approval. You will be able to sign in once an operator activates it.
auth.oauth.google_org_rejected: This school registration was not approved. Contact support if you believe this is a mistake.
auth.oauth.too_many_requests: Too many sign-in attempts from this network. Wait a few minutes and try again.
platform.loginDescription: Cross-tenant console. Not for school staff or students.
platform.loginErrorGeneric: Could not sign in. Check platform credentials and server configuration.
courses.staffIntro: Create and publish courses for your organization.
courses.studentIntro: Enroll in published courses or continue where you left off.
courses.createShellHint: You can add modules and lessons after creating the shell.
courses.emptyHint: Create a course to add modules, lessons, and assessments.
courses.notEnrolledYet: You are not enrolled in any course yet.
courses.catalogEmpty: No published courses are open for enrollment right now.
courses.noModulesStaff: No modules yet. Use Edit structure to add some.
courses.attentionOne: %s published assessment may need attention (low mean or participation).
courses.attentionMany: %s published assessments may need attention (low mean or participation).
courses.editor.confirmDeleteModule: Delete this module and all its lessons?
courses.editor.fileTypesHint: PDF, images, plain text, or Markdown — max 15 MB.
courses.grading.introRollups: Quizzes roll into continuous assessment (CA); exams into the exam bucket. Tag assessments with semester 1–3 for rollups.
courses.grading.toastWeightsSum: Continuous + exam weights must sum to 1 (e.g. 0.4 and 0.6).
courses.grading.toastCreditsInvalid: Credit hours must be a non-negative number.
courses.cohortLink.title: Classes taking this course
courses.cohortLink.intro: Link homeroom classes so teachers can assign assessments to specific groups. Students still enroll individually; targeting uses class membership.
courses.departmentLink.title: Departments taking this course
courses.departmentLink.intro: Link academic departments so you can target assessments by department. Students still enroll individually; visibility uses department affiliation.
assessments.staffIntroTeacher: Assessments on courses you author or where you instruct a linked class or department. New quizzes and exams are created inside a course. Use filters to narrow the list below.
assessments.staffIntroAdminHe: Filter by academic department. Link departments to courses, then target assessments to specific departments from the assessment editor.
assessments.staffIntroAdminK12: Filter by class or academic year. Link classes to courses, then target assessments to specific homerooms or form groups.
assessments.staffOutcomesNote: Outcomes are per course (class mean, participation, needs attention). Use the buttons here or open a course Assessments page.
assessments.parentIntroHe: Published work for courses your linked students take. Department-targeted items appear only for affiliated students.
assessments.parentIntroK12: Published quizzes and exams for courses your linked students are enrolled in. Class-targeted items only appear when the student is in the assigned group.
assessments.parentEmpty: Nothing here yet — ask a school admin to link your account to a student, or confirm the student is enrolled in courses with assessments.
assessments.studentIntroHe: Published work for your enrolled courses. Some items may be limited to your department — ask faculty if something is missing.
assessments.studentIntroK12: Published quizzes and exams for courses you are enrolled in. Some may be limited to your class or form group.
assessments.studentEmpty: Nothing here yet — enroll in a course with assessments.
assessments.toolbarTeacherNoCourses: You don't have a course where you can add assessments yet. You need to author a course or be linked as instructor on a class or department tied to a course. Ask an admin if you expect access.
assessments.toolbarAdminLead: No courses in this school yet.
assessments.integrity.stayInWindow: Switching tabs or windows may be recorded. Stay in this window until you submit.
assessments.integrity.lockdownPaste: Right-click and copy/cut/paste shortcuts outside answer fields are restricted.
assessments.integrity.leftBanner: You left this assessment — this may be logged for your instructor.
assessments.delivery.noteSecureOnline: This assessment may log when you leave the window or switch tabs.
assessments.delivery.noteLockdown: Strict mode: limited copy/paste and context menu outside answers; leaving the window may be logged.
assessments.enrollToSeePrompt: Enroll in this course to see assessments.
assessments.instructorPausedHint: Your instructor has paused new attempts. If you were already started, use Resume.
assessments.formativeIntegrityNote: No new signals are accepted while the assessment is formative; older rows may remain from a previous mode.
assessments.outcomesExplainer: Score % uses (total ÷ max) × 100 per submitted attempt with a positive max (Scored attempts); raw attempt counts include all submitted rows. Particip. % is distinct students with at least one submission ÷ course enrollments (%s enrolled). Flags highlight published assessments with unusually low mean (≥5 scored attempts) or low participation (≥8 enrolled). Use Needs attention to narrow to those rows. Integrity counts are all logged events for the assessment.
assessments.emptyOutcomesNoAttention: No assessments need attention with the current visibility and kind filters.
assessments.excusedAuditNote: Excused rows stay in the log for audit but no longer count toward gradebook warnings or outcome totals.
assessments.integrityNoEvents: No integrity events match these filters.
assessments.integrityHideExcusedHint: Hide excused events (show only active signals)
assessments.editStudentPreviewLink: Student preview (blocked for staff — copy link for student)
assessments.editor.deliveryFormative: Formative — no focus logging or lockdown UI
assessments.editor.deliverySecure: Secure online — logs tab/window leave; optional fullscreen; visible warnings
assessments.editor.deliveryLockdown: High lockdown — same as secure plus no right-click; copy/cut/paste blocked outside answers
assessments.editor.deliveryHelp: Integrity signals are stored for staff review; they do not replace a dedicated lockdown browser or invigilation.
assessments.editor.lockAttemptsHelp: Blocks starting a fresh attempt. Students with an in-progress draft can still save answers and submit.
assessments.editor.shuffleMcq: Shuffle MCQ answer options for students
assessments.editor.showAnswers: Let students see correct answers / keys after they submit (quizzes & exams)
assessments.editor.maxAttemptsHint: Additional tries after this need approval when enabled below.
assessments.editor.retakeApproval: Require teacher/admin approval for attempts beyond the max
assessments.editor.assignDepartmentsHelp: Leave all unchecked so every enrolled student sees this assessment. Check departments to limit visibility (each must be linked to this course; you must be chair or instructor on that department).
assessments.editor.assignClassesHelp: Leave all unchecked so every enrolled student sees this assessment. Check specific classes to limit visibility (you must teach each class).
assessments.editor.linkDepartmentsHint: Link departments to this course on the course edit page to target this assessment.
assessments.editor.linkClassesHint: Link classes to this course on the course edit page to target this assessment at specific homerooms.
assessments.editor.pdfImportError: Could not generate questions (check API key & PDF).
assessments.editor.aiPdfHelp: Set GOOGLE_AI_API_KEY or GEMINI_API_KEY. PDF text is extracted server-side.
assessments.editor.questionsBlurb: Compose items for this quiz or exam. The take experience uses a clear card layout for students; this studio matches that structure where it helps you preview choices and prompts.
assessments.editor.noQuestionsComposer: No questions yet. Use the composer to add your first item.
assessments.editor.questionComposerBlurb: Pick a type, write the prompt, then complete the fields below. Multiple-choice options mirror the student take layout.
assessments.builder.mcq.label: Multiple choice
assessments.builder.short.label: Short answer
assessments.builder.long.label: Long answer
assessments.builder.tf.label: True / false
assessments.builder.dd.label: Drag & drop
assessments.builder.formula.label: Formula
assessments.builder.essay.label: Rich essay

assessments.editor.mcqIntro: Each option uses the same editor as the question. Select which option is correct.
assessments.editor.tfCorrect: Correct answer is True (uncheck for False)
assessments.editor.formulaCorrectLabel: Correct LaTeX (normalized match: spacing / case ignored)
assessments.schedule.title: Assessment schedule
assessments.schedule.intro: Continuous assessment (quiz) open/due times and exam session windows feed the school dashboard calendar and in-app reminders. Remove all rows and save to clear the schedule (stored open/due fields on the assessment are cleared too).
assessments.schedule.emptyHint: No schedule rows yet. Add a row to publish dates to the calendar.
assessments.schedule.toastStartDateAllDa: Row %s: start date required when all-day is checked.
assessments.schedule.toastEndExamAllDay: Row %s: end date required for exam windows when all-day.
assessments.schedule.toastEndExam: Row %s: end date/time required for exam windows.
assessments.schedule.customPlaceholder: Overrides default title for this row
assessments.take.attemptsLockedDefault: This assessment is closed to new attempts. Contact your instructor if you need access.
assessments.take.retryHint: You can try loading the assessment again.
assessments.take.timeUpBanner: Time is up — submitting now (draft answers are included).
assessments.take.legendDots: Amber = current · Green tint = answered · Outline = not answered
assessments.take.noQuestions: This assessment has no questions yet.
assessments.take.ddBroken: This drag-and-drop question is not configured correctly. Contact your instructor.
assessments.take.tipShift: Tip: use Shift + ← / → to move between questions when not typing in a field.
assessments.take.unansweredOne: 1 question still has no selected or written response.
assessments.take.unansweredMany: %s questions still have no selected or written response.
assessments.take.unansweredFooter: You can submit anyway; unanswered items may receive no points.
assessments.take.leaveConfirm: Leave this attempt? Draft answers are saved. Come back to finish, or submit when you are ready.
assessments.results.title: Results · %s
assessments.results.allAttemptsUsed: You have used all %s allowed attempt%s.
assessments.results.answerReviewOff: Your instructor has turned off answer review for this activity. You can still see your score and any written feedback above each question when provided.
assessments.results.guardianHidden: Answer keys and model solutions are hidden in guardian view. See overall scores above or ask the school for a formal report.
assessments.gbPage.title: Gradebook · %s
assessments.grantExtra.help: Lets this student start one more session when they have used all allowed tries (same for quizzes and exams). Optional note is stored on the retake record.
assessments.discardDraft.confirm: Delete this in-progress draft? The student can start again if attempts allow.
assessments.excuse.submissionHelp: Marks all integrity signals for this submission as excused. The log is kept for audit.
assessments.studentRetake.requestSent: Request sent. Your teacher will review it.
assessments.studentRetake.canRetryFromLi: You can start another attempt from the assessment list (up to %s submitted attempt%s).
assessments.studentRetake.usedAll: You have used all %s allowed attempt%s for this activity.
assessments.studentRetake.pendingBody: A teacher or admin needs to approve before you can try again.
assessments.studentRetake.submittedTimes: You have submitted %s time%s (limit %s). Ask your instructor for another attempt.
assessments.studentRetake.needAnother: Need another try? You still have built-in attempts left — open the assessment again from the list.
assessments.staffRetake.title: Retake requests
assessments.staffRetake.intro: Approve student requests here, or use Grant extra attempt on a gradebook row to add an approved retake without a student request (quizzes and exams).
assessments.staffRetake.placeholder: Optional note to student (shown if denied)
assessments.itemIx.title: Item analysis
assessments.itemIx.intro: Per-question stats from submitted attempts only. Mean % uses the effective score on each answer (manual override when present). “Full credit %” applies to auto-graded question types (MCQ, true/false, short answer, formula, drag-drop). MCQ / true-false / drag-drop include response distributions.
assessments.itemIx.poolBanner: This assessment uses question pools. Students may see different items; rows marked as pool items can have fewer responses than the number of submissions.
assessments.itemIx.discriminationIntro: p̂ overall is the proportion correct among attempts with a gradable answer; r_pb is the Pearson correlation between correct (0/1) and total score % (point-biserial-style). Students are split into the lower and upper ~27% by total score % when there are at least four scored attempts; then p̂ low / p̂ high and D = p̂ high − p̂ low (−1…+1) summarize tail contrast. Negative D suggests the item may be misfitting or keyed wrong. Pool-based forms can make tails noisy if many students never saw an item.
